meta.title: Pricing
meta.description: Transparent pricing for support retainers, hourly services, and project engagements.

intro.label: Pricing
intro.heading: Transparent by design.
intro.sub: Three engagement models. No hidden fees. All prices are exclusive of VAT — EU reverse charge applies for cross-border B2B transactions.

categories.title: Support Retainer
categories.model: Annual flat fee · per system instance
categories.desc: Third-level escalation for industrial software systems. Covers incident investigation, root cause analysis, workaround guidance, documentation, and remote consultation — across all covered instances.
categories.excludes: ⚠️ Deployment to production systems, on-site visits, and new feature development are excluded and governed separately.

subtypes.title: Support of Legacy Systems
subtypes.desc: For systems with limited or no original documentation. Investigation requires reverse-engineering, log analysis, and source-level archaeology. Higher rates reflect the additional overhead of working with undocumented, end-of-life, or vendor-unsupported software.

sla.definition: System fully offline, data loss risk
Significant data gap or integration failure

notes: Included hours are pooled across all instances within a tier — a quiet instance offsets a busy one.
Excess hours beyond the annual pool are billed at €150/hour with prior written approval.
Instances may be on different tiers within the same agreement.
Total pooled hours across all instances and tiers cannot exceed 2,500 hours per year (10 hours/day × 250 working days) — the maximum annual capacity of one engineer.
Business hours: Mon–Fri 09:00–18:00 CET, excluding Bulgarian and EU public holidays.

subtypes.title: Support of New Products
subtypes.desc: Applies only to systems delivered by Imbra.Soft and covered by full arc42 architecture documentation. Same service conditions as legacy support — lower rates reflect the structured codebase, modern tooling, and complete documentation available from day one.

sla.definition: System fully offline, data loss risk
Significant data gap or integration failure

notes: Included hours are pooled across all instances within a tier — a quiet instance offsets a busy one.
Excess hours beyond the annual pool are billed at €150/hour with prior written approval.
Instances may be on different tiers within the same agreement.
Total pooled hours across all instances and tiers cannot exceed 2,500 hours per year.
Business hours: Mon–Fri 09:00–18:00 CET, excluding Bulgarian and EU public holidays.

categories.title: Hourly Services
categories.desc: Time-billed engagements for reactive and investigative work. Applicable when scope cannot be defined in advance — emergency fixes, incident analysis, or technical exploration.

subtypes.title: Hotfix & Emergency
subtypes.desc: Analysis, preparation, and delivery of patches, configuration corrections, or emergency fixes. All artifacts are tested in-house before delivery.
subtypes.excludes: ⚠️ Production deployment is carried out by the client.
subtypes.outofhours: 1.5× multiplier — evenings after 18:00 CET, weekends, public holidays
subtypes.expenses: The hourly rate covers all standard expenses. Exceptional costs exceeding €1,000 (travel, third-party licences, specialised tooling) are borne by the client with prior written agreement.

subtypes.title: Exploration & Spikes
subtypes.desc: A Spike is a time-boxed investigation used to reduce technical uncertainty before committing to a full project. The outcome is a written finding — not production code. Common uses: feasibility assessment, architecture options analysis, protocol compatibility study, or vendor evaluation.
subtypes.excludes: ⚠️ A Spike does not produce deployable software. If the investigation leads to a project, a separate Statement of Work is required.
subtypes.expenses: All expenses incurred during the engagement (travel, third-party licences, specialised tooling) are borne by the client.

categories.title: Project Engagement
categories.desc: Refactoring, new features, migrations, and integrations. Each project is governed by a Statement of Work agreed in writing before commencement, defining scope, deliverables, timeline, and acceptance criteria.

models.name: Fixed Price
models.desc: Applied when scope is clearly defined. Scope changes require a written Change Request.

models.name: Time & Materials
models.desc: Applied for refactoring or development work where the full scope cannot be determined in advance. Weekly timesheets provided for client approval.

terms: All agreements are governed by Bulgarian law. Full terms are included in each engagement contract.

cta.heading: Not sure which model fits?
cta.sub: Describe your situation and we will recommend the right engagement type.
cta.label: Get in touch
